bead.title: Design a CRM
bead.description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>CRM-001</governing>
</context-digest>

Sparse intake request: "design a CRM".

Interpretation:
- Core intent: create the governed planning/design work needed for a new CRM product.
- Artifact layer affected: frame and design, with downstream decomposition into execution-ready work.
- Explicit constraints: none beyond high autonomy.

Current repo search found no existing CRM artifact stack or matching open bead. The HELIX repo's governing PRD is about HELIX itself, so a separate escalation bead records the repo-scope conflict. Until that is resolved, treat this as greenfield CRM planning work managed through HELIX.

Working assumptions for downstream beads:
- B2B web CRM for a small-to-mid-size sales team
- MVP includes contacts, accounts, leads/opportunities, tasks/activities, notes, pipeline visibility, and basic reporting
- Multi-user SaaS posture; implementation architecture still to be designed
bead.acceptance:
1. A governed CRM artifact stack is created through HELIX, starting with frame artifacts and continuing into design and decomposition work.
2. Downstream beads use provisional spec-id CRM-001 until authoritative CRM frame artifacts establish canonical IDs.
3. Scope assumptions created from the sparse intake are explicit rather than hidden.
